title: Speaking & Listening
objective: Students will explore speaking & listening concepts including Identifying Reasons Behind a Speaker's Points, Paraphrasing What You Hear.

lessons.title: Identifying Reasons Behind a Speaker's Points

steps.content: Let's explore Identifying Reasons Behind a Speaker's Points! Have you ever wondered about this?
Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker provides to support particular points, evaluating whether the reasoning is logical and the evidence is relevant
Let's practice together! Listen to a speaker's argument or presentation and list the specific reasons and evidence provided to support each main point
When you listens to someone making an argument or giving reasons for something, can they tell you whether the reasons given actually support what the person is claiming?

Great job! Today we learned about Identifying Reasons Behind a Speaker's Points. Listen to a speaker's argument or presentation and list the specific reasons and evidence provided to support each main point Evaluate whether the reasons a speaker gives are logical and whether the evidence cited is relevant to the claim being made
Today we're going to learn about Paraphrasing What You Hear. Get ready for an adventure!
Paraphrase portions of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, capturing the key ideas accurately in one's own words
Let's practice together! Listen to a passage read aloud and restate the main idea and key details in own words without looking at the original text
If you listens to a short talk or watches a video clip about a topic, can they explain the main points back to you in their own words?

Great job! Today we learned about Paraphrasing What You Hear. Listen to a passage read aloud and restate the main idea and key details in own words without looking at the original text Paraphrase information from a short video, podcast, or presentation, identifying the central message and supporting details
